description: Set up Antora documentation and GitHub Pages publishing for redis-vl-dotnet, migrate and expand the current docs into a canonical site, and ensure each major feature area has working example coverage with environment-variable-based provider configuration.

userStories.title: Scaffold the Antora site structure
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the repository to contain a working Antora scaffold so that documentation has a stable buildable structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add the Antora playbook, component metadata, and navigation files needed to build the site
Create Antora sections for getting-started, core-features, extensions, examples, cli, testing, and reference
Document the local docs build command in the repository
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add GitHub Pages publishing workflow
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want GitHub Actions to build and publish the Antora site so that documentation is deployed automatically from GitHub.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that builds the Antora site on the default branch
Configure the workflow to publish the generated site to GitHub Pages using supported GitHub Actions deployment steps
Document required repository Pages settings and workflow permissions
Ensure failed docs builds stop publishing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add docs and example quality gates
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want repeatable docs validation commands and CI gates so that published documentation does not drift away from the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a docs validation path that builds the Antora site in CI
Add an example validation sweep that compiles each example project
Document the local validation commands for docs and examples
Fail CI when Antora navigation or documented example builds break
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate repository entry-point docs into Antora
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the current top-level documentation migrated into Antora so that there is one canonical documentation entry point.
acceptanceCriteria: Migrate or rewrite the current README and core docs entry-point content into Antora landing and overview pages
Add repository-root links that point users to the Antora docs entry point
Mark or remove superseded duplicate docs so there is not more than one canonical source for the same guidance
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document installation and environment setup
userStories.description: As a new user, I want clear setup documentation so that I can install prerequisites, start Redis, and configure environment variables without trial-and-error.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a getting-started page covering .NET SDK requirements, Redis prerequisites, and local Redis startup commands
Document REDIS_VL_REDIS_URL, cluster, Sentinel, and provider environment variables where applicable
Include copy-paste-ready commands for the default local setup flow
Link the setup page to example and testing documentation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document core schema and index workflows
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want schema and index lifecycle documentation so that I can define indexes, create them, inspect them, reuse them, and remove them confidently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Antora pages for SearchSchema, IndexDefinition, field definitions, YAML schema loading, and index lifecycle operations
Document JSON and HASH storage behavior plus advanced index options supported by the codebase
Link the schema/index docs to the primary working example for this feature area
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document document lifecycle and update workflows
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want document lifecycle documentation so that I can load, fetch, partially update, clear, and drop documents safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add docs for JSON and HASH document load, fetch, delete, clear, and drop flows
Document JSON partial updates and HASH partial updates including limitations and key resolution behavior
Ensure the document lifecycle docs point to a working example that demonstrates create, fetch, update, and cleanup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document query and aggregation workflows
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want each query type documented so that I can choose and apply the correct search and aggregation API without reading internal command builders.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pages for FilterQuery, TextQuery, CountQuery, VectorQuery, VectorRangeQuery, HybridQuery, AggregateHybridQuery, MultiVectorQuery, and aggregation workflows
Document pagination, batch helpers, typed result mapping, return fields, and runtime vector options
Link each major query area to a working example or a clearly identified example section
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document higher-level cache and workflow APIs
userStories.description: As an application developer, I want documentation for caches, routers, and message history so that I can build AI-adjacent workflows with the library.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pages for EmbeddingsCache, SemanticCache, SemanticRouter, MessageHistory, and SemanticMessageHistory
Document thresholds, filters, recency behavior, vectorizer usage, and the obsolete embedding-generator shim where relevant
Link the cache and workflow docs to working examples for those feature areas
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document extension packages and provider-backed examples
userStories.description: As a developer using provider integrations, I want extension package documentation and runnable examples so that I can configure OpenAI, Hugging Face, and Cohere using environment variables instead of embedded secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add docs for vectorizer abstractions, reranker abstractions, OpenAI vectorizer, Hugging Face vectorizer, and Cohere reranker packages
Document OPENAI_API_KEY, HF_TOKEN, and COHERE_API_KEY as required environment variables where applicable
Ensure each provider area has a working example project and setup instructions that consume credentials from environment variables
Document expected behavior when required provider credentials are missing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document CLI workflows
userStories.description: As an operator or developer, I want CLI documentation so that I can manage indexes and schemas from the terminal without reading the source code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CLI docs for build/install, command discovery, index lifecycle commands, and schema validate/show flows
Document required connection arguments and runnable local command examples
Include troubleshooting notes for connection problems and missing Redis modules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align major feature examples with the docs site
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want one working example per major feature area and docs pages that link to them so that readers can move from explanation to runnable code immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirm or add working example coverage for core schema/index/document workflows, query/aggregation workflows, cache workflows, message-history/router workflows, provider vectorizer workflows, reranker workflows, and CLI workflows
Update example docs so each major feature area has a clear entry point from Antora navigation
Document required Redis modules and provider env vars for each example where applicable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Organize completed planning artifacts
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want completed planning artifacts moved into the completed-prds area so that active planning files remain easy to find.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or preserve a completed-prds folder for superseded planning artifacts
Move the previous root prd.json and superseded PRD markdown files into completed-prds while leaving the active docs PRD in tasks
Retain historical planning files without deleting their content
Typecheck passes
